name: ElmAdmin

aliases.name: RouteParams
aliases.type: { path : String.String, pathParams : Dict.Dict String.String String.String, queryParams : Dict.Dict String.String (List.List String.String) }

values.name: admin
values.comment:
Bootstraps your admin application.

    admin
        { title = "My Admin"
        , init = init
        , update = update
        , subscriptions = subscriptions
        , options = [ preferDarkMode ]
        , pages =
            [ A.page "Home" Home.page
            , A.external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"
            , A.resources "Users"
                { index = Users.index
                , show = Users.show
                , create = Users.create
                , update = Users.update
                }
            , A.group "Workspaces"
                { main = Workspaces.index
                , items =
                    [ A.single "Archive" Workspaces.archive
                    ]
                }
            ]
        }
values.type: { title : String.String, init : flags -> Browser.Navigation.Key -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), update : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> msg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, options : List.List (ElmAdmin.Options -> ElmAdmin.Options), pages : List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) } -> ElmAdmin.ElmAdmin flags model msg

values.name: darkModeClass
values.comment:
Sets the class added to the DOM when dark mode is on.

Tip: If you're using tailwind's `dark:` variants you might want to set this to `"dark"`.

values.name: darkTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on dark mode.

values.name: disabled
values.comment:
Conditionally disable nav links and their pages. They won't be accessible even through urls.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> disable (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: dynamic
values.comment:
Dynamic nav link label.

    single "User" User.show
        |> dynamic
            (\{ pathParams } model ->
                Dict.get ":userId" pathParams
                    |> Maybe.andThen (username model)
            )
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> String.String) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: external
values.comment:
Used for creating external links in your sidebar.

    external "Docs" "https://package.elm-lang.org/"

values.name: folderGroup
values.comment:
A group that shows its items only if one of them is the current active path.

    folderGroup "Workspaces"
        Workspace.index
        [ single "New" Workspaces.new
        , url "Show" Workspaces.show
        , url "Update" Workspaces.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: fullPage
values.comment:
Creates a page with it's own Elm architecture.

  - Your `init` will run every time the page gets initialized.

  - Your `update` will run _after_ your main update (and any commands will be batched).

  - Your `subscriptions` will be active only while this page is active.

```
usersIndex : Page model msg
usersIndex =
    fullPage "/users"
        { title = \routeParams model -> ...
        , init = \routeParams model -> ...
        , update = \routeParams model -> ...
        , subscriptions = \routeParams model -> ...
        , view = \routeParams model -> ...
        }
```
values.type: String.String -> { title : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> String.String, init : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), update : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> msg -> model -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, view : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Html.Html msg } -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg

values.name: group
values.comment:
Used for creating grouped pages. Note that the "group" is also a page and if it is hidden or disabled by any means, then the whole group will follow.

    group "Workspace"
        Workspace.index
        [ single "New" Workspace.create
        , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: hidden
values.comment:
Conditionally hide nav links.

    single "Roles" Roles.page
        |> hidden (\_ model -> not (userIsAdmin model))
values.type: (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Basics.Bool) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: lightTheme
values.comment: Sets the theme used on light mode.

values.name: page
values.comment:
Creates a page.

    usersIndex : Page model msg
    usersIndex =
        viewPage "/users" "Users"
            (\routeParams model ->
                div [] [ ... ]
            )
values.type: String.String -> String.String -> (ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> Html.Html msg) -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg

values.name: params
values.comment:
Manually pass in url params to generate fixed nav links for pages that take in pathParams.

    single "User" User.show
        |> params [ ( ":userId", "123" ) ]
values.type: List.List ( String.String, String.String ) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: preferDarkMode
values.comment: Starts the admin on dark mode.

values.name: resourcePage
values.comment:
Creates a `fullPage` with quick access to a "resource".

    usersShow : Page model msg
    usersShow =
        resourcePage "/users/:userId"
            { resource = \{ pathParams } model ->
                pathParams
                    |> Dict.get ":userId"
                    |> Maybe.andThen (getUser model)
            , title = \routeParams model user ->
                user
                    |> Maybe.map .name
                    |> Maybe.withDefault "…"
            , init = \routeParams model user -> ...
            , update = \routeParams model user -> ...
            , subscriptions = \routeParams model user -> ...
            , view = \routeParams model user -> ...
            }
values.type: String.String -> { resource : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource, title : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> String.String, init : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), update : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> msg -> model -> resource -> ( model, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg ), subscriptions : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> Platform.Sub.Sub msg, view : ElmAdmin.RouteParams -> model -> resource -> Html.Html msg } -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg

values.name: single
values.comment:
Used for creating a single page with a nav link.

    single "User" User.page

Note that all page links with params will only appear if they are already present on the current page path.

    -- User.page { path = "/users/:userId", ... }

    single "User" User.page

    -- This page link will appear if the user is on "/settings/:userId"
    -- But if the user is on "/", no ":userId" is available so the link will disappear.
values.type: String.String -> ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: url
values.comment:
Used for creating pages that will not appear as nav links but are still accessible through their direct url.

    url Home.page
values.type: ElmAdmin.Page model msg -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg

values.name: visualGroup
values.comment:
Creates a visual group (The group label itself is not a nav link).

Tip: This can be useful if you want to disable whole sections of your application but you don't want that to be related to one specific root page.

    visualGroup "Workspace"
        [ single "New" Workspace.create
        , hidden "Update" Workspace.update
        ]
values.type: String.String -> List.List (ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg) -> ElmAdmin.NavigationItem model msg
